steps.title: Identify the target meaning
steps.content: The passage says Gogol must “tip his head back in order to see” the kite flying high in the sky. This describes a physical movement of the head—specifically, tilting or leaning it backward to look up.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
The underlined word needs to mean tilt or incline — Gogol must angle his head backward to see the kite high in the sky. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Lean: Gogol must "lean his head back" — lean means to tilt or incline, which fits the physical action of tilting the head backward to look up. ✓
  • Choice B — Create: Gogol must "create his head back" — create means to make or produce something; it cannot describe a physical head movement. ✗
  • Choice C — Pay: Gogol must "pay his head back" — pay means to give money or settle a debt, entirely unrelated to a physical body position. ✗
  • Choice D — Teach: Gogol must "teach his head back" — teach means to instruct, which makes no sense as a physical action. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
